blocks.content: Tuesday after school. Daniel caught me in the hall between the gym and the south stairwell. He wasn't loud this time. That was the difference.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: You think that was funny. What happened in the hall with your boy.
He meant Jonathan. Jonathan hadn't even done anything — he'd just looked at Daniel a certain way and Daniel had walked off. But that's the kind of thing that costs Daniel a lot apparently.
He wasn't going to throw a punch in the hallway. He was too smart for that now — he'd learned something from Episode 1 even if he'd never say so. But he wanted me to flinch. That was the whole thing.

blocks.text: didn't flinch. He'd decided that before the conversation started.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm not in what happened with Jonathan.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: You're always in it. You think you're not.
He didn't move for a second. And then he stepped back. Not far — just enough to let me pass. His jaw was set in a way that said this wasn't over. I walked through.

blocks.prompt: Daniel stepped back. But he didn't leave.

options.label: That's not a win. That's just a pause. He'll be back.
He held his ground. That's all he could do. The rest isn't his to control.
